page.content: The report analyzes the effects of various antidepressants on Cx43 expression and function across multiple studies. Amitriptyline, a TCA, increases both mRNA and protein levels of Cx43 in primary cultures of rat cortical astrocytes with a treatment dose of 25 μM for 48 hours, as noted by Morioka et al., 2014. Clomipramine, also a TCA, increases Cx43 protein in primary cultures of rat cortical astrocytes with a treatment dose of 10 μM for 48 hours, per Morioka et al., 2014. Fluoxetine, classified as an SSRI, shows an increase in protein in a rat in vivo prefrontal cortex at a dosage of 20 mg/kg i.p.

Amitriptyline, a TCA, increases both mRNA and protein levels of Cx43 in primary cultures of rat cortical astrocytes with a
treatment dose of 25 μM for 48 hours, as noted by Morioka et al., 2014. Clomipramine, also a TCA, increases Cx43 protein in
primary cultures of rat cortical astrocytes with a treatment dose of 10 μM for 48 hours, per Morioka et al., 2014. Fluoxetine,
classified as an SSRI, shows an increase in protein in a rat in vivo prefrontal cortex at a dosage of 20 mg/kg i.p.

page.content: for 21 days according to Fatemi et al., 2008. In another study, it increases both mRNA and protein in a human astrocytoma cell line with doses of 10 and 20 μg/ml for 24 hours, as shown by Mostafavi et al., 2008. Additionally, Fluoxetine at 10 mg/kg for 21 days results in increased protein but no effect on gap junctions in a rat in vivo prefrontal cortex, as noted by Sun et al., 2012. Duloxetine, an SNRI, elevates mRNA and protein levels without affecting gap junctions in a rat in vivo prefrontal cortex at 10 mg/kg for 21 days, also per Sun et al., 2012. Fluvoxamine, another SSRI, increases protein in primary cultures of rat cortical astrocytes with a 25 μM dose for 48 hours, according to Morioka et al., 2014. In Table 2, the US Collaborative sample consisted of 59 patients with a median age of 9.9 (3.3 – 15.9), while the French, Belgian, and CIBMTR cohorts had 87 patients (median age 9.5 (2–22)), 50 patients (median age 7.5 (0.9 – 23)), and 67 patients (median age 10 (2–27)), respectively. Disease recurrence rates were 8.5% in the US, 8% in France, 10% in Belgium, and 13% in CIBMTR. Deaths were recorded at 7% in both the US and France, 4% in Belgium, and 4.5% in CIBMTR. Event-free survival rates were 85% for the US and France, 86% for Belgium, and 82% for CIBMTR. Patients with chronic GvHD were 12% in the US, 12.6% in France, 20% in Belgium, and 22% in CIBMTR.
